reforms.description: The Department of Regional Economic Expansion (DREE) was established to stimulate employment growth in weaker regions through incentive grants for private investment and infrastructure assistance. Additionally, the 8th May 1972 Federal budget introduced measures to accelerate the write-off of costs for machinery and equipment used in manufacturing, alongside a reduction in corporate tax rates for manufacturing and processing profits.
reforms.package_name: Regional Economic Expansion and Manufacturing Support
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to stimulate investment and employment, which are expected to enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Both reforms have been implemented as part of the federal budget and regional development strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of DREE and significant tax measures represent structural changes aimed at enhancing regional economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The most direct recent attack on the problem has been taken through the range of co-ordinated activities of the Department of Regional Economic Expansion (DREE), which was established in April 1969.

reforms.description: The Federal Government has rapidly expanded manpower training and mobility programmes, significantly increasing funding from C$ 108 million to C$ 297 million over three years, aimed at improving educational and training standards in low-performing regions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in funding for training programmes is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby improving productivity and economic outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The expansion of manpower training programmes has been successfully implemented as part of the federal government's initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant increase in funding represents a notable but not structural change in the approach to workforce development.
reforms.source_quote: Policies of manpower training and mobility have been rapidly expanded in recent years; in the 1970-1971 fiscal year, about C$ 297 million was spent on Federal manpower training and mobility programmes, compared to about C$ 108 million only three years earlier.

reforms.description: The Federal Winter Job Expansion Plan was announced to combat seasonal unemployment, aiming to create over 140,000 jobs through various direct job-creation projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The job creation initiative is expected to reduce unemployment and stimulate economic activity during the winter months.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been implemented and is actively creating jobs as intended.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant in its job creation goals, it is a targeted initiative rather than a structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Winter Job Expansion Plan to help combat seasonal unemployment was announced in December 1972.
